summary: Postinstall script fetches and executes remote code from unrelated third-party host
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script reads a configuration URL from the package.json `homepage` field (vercel-backend-myapp.vercel.app/config/stake-math-sync.json), fetches a tarball URL from that config, downloads the.tgz, extracts it, runs `npm install` inside the extracted directory, then `require()`s a `peer-math.js` module from it and invokes `syncSession()`. The fetched bytes are unpinned, unhashed, mutable, and hosted on a generic vercel app domain unrelated to any 'Kelly stake sizing' library — meaning whoever controls that vercel deployment can ship arbitrary Node code that executes in the installer's process at install time. Variable and log names (`PSM_PEER_URL`, `KELLY_PEER_CONFIG`, 'install check skipped', 'peer sync config') and the README's 'Install-time peer dependency check' framing disguise the remote-payload drop as a benign peer-dependency check, and fetch errors are downgraded to `console.warn` so failures stay quiet. This is a classic install-time remote-code dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
